title: dForge Module Reports
description: Report definitions for a dForge module: a map of report code → report definition. Reports are not listed in manifest.json; the install pipeline picks up ui/reports.json automatically.

datasets.description: Map of dataset code → dataset definition. Panels reference datasets by code via `datasetCd`.

parameters.description: Report-level parameters (alternative to per-dataset `params`). Map of param code → param definition.

vizType.description: Visualization type. (Older packages may use `metric` for `kpi`.)

datasetCd.description: Dataset code (key in the parent `datasets` map)

config.description:
Viz-type-specific configuration.
- chart: { chartType ('bar'|'horizontalBar'|'stackedBar'|'combo'|'line'|'area'|'pie'|'doughnut'|'scatter'|'bubble'|'funnel'|'heatmap'), categoryCol, valueCol, agg, seriesCol?, sizeCol?, chartSize? ('sm'|'m'|'l'|'xl'), clickAction?, showTrend?, series? }. `series` (a single object OR an array) declares CROSS-SOURCE overlay series on bar/horizontalBar/line/area, each { source?, categoryCol, valueCol, agg, label? }; `source` names a sibling dataset code (omit = the panel's own dataset). Overlays share one category axis (union of category values; bar gaps fill 0, line/area gap with null).
- kpi: { metrics: [ metric, ... ] }. Each metric is EITHER an aggregation metric { column, agg ('sum'|'avg'|'min'|'max'|'count'), display?, label?, target?, min?, max?, icon?, sparklineDimension? } OR a FORMULA metric { formula (e.g. '[won] / [all] * 100'), inputs: [ { alias, column, agg, source? } ], format?: { style?: 'number'|'percent', decimals? }, display?, label? }. A formula input's `source` names a sibling dataset code for CROSS-DATASET metrics (omit = the panel's own dataset). Auto format (omit `format.style`) inherits the first input column's own formatter (currency/grouping).
- table: { groupRules, aggregations, colorRules }.

caption.description: Display label for the dataset (used in panel headings if no `title`)

datasetType.description: Q = SQL/entity query, S = stored procedure

columnsDef.description: Optional column metadata overrides: field code → { label, visible, ... }

columns.description: Column codes to select (supports dot navigation: 'customer.name')

rollup.description: Hierarchy rollup: roll the flat leaf result up a self-referencing tree so parent rows carry the SUM of their descendants. Emits one row per tree node (leaf + ancestor), plus '_level' (depth from root) and '_isLeaf'. Measures must be additively-rollable (physical additive columns or LINEAR formulas of them, e.g. closing = opening + turnover); non-linear measures (ratios, products) are not valid and need a computed-row feature instead.

tree.description: Entity code that owns the hierarchy (e.g. 'account').

treeKey.description: Primary-key column of the tree entity (e.g. 'account_id').

parentField.description: Self-referencing parent FK on the tree entity; roots have it NULL (e.g. 'parent_account_id').

leafKey.description: Column on the query entity identifying the tree node a row belongs to (e.g. 'account_id' on balance_register).

measures.description: Value column codes (on the query entity) to SUM into ancestors. Must be additively-rollable.

labels.description: Node-describing columns, expressed as the report column keys ('account.account_code'). Sourced from the tree entity's physical column (the segment after the dot); emitted under the same key so the report layout is unchanged.

order.description: Tree-entity column code for pre-order sort (e.g. 'account_code'), resolved through metadata to its physical column. Assumes hierarchy-sortable codes; defaults to treeKey.

fieldTypeCd.description: Field type code (see settings.schema.json for the common list)

orderNum.description: Display order of the parameter in the report's parameter form. Read by ReportRegistrar (paramDef.OrderNum); falls back to declaration order when omitted.

required.description: Whether the user must provide a value (older modules also use `isRequired`)

isRequired.description: Alternate spelling of `required` used by some modules. Prefer `required` for new code.

default.description: Default value applied if the user doesn't provide one

link.description: Entity link for lookup-type params (alternative to nesting under `params.link`). Shape: { entity: '<code>', otherKey?: '<column>' }.

params.description: Field-type-specific params (e.g. lookup → { link: { entity, otherKey } })

filter.description: Canonical dForge filter expression — same shape as data_views.json filter. Either `{c, o, v}` or `{g, i: [...]}`.
